stories.title: Born in south Lebanon, displaced to Beirut, two grandmothers reflect on Israeli invasions
stories.description: They grew up amid olive groves in southern Lebanon. The son of one married the other's daughter. Now they're living temporarily in a vacant building in central Beirut, displaced many times.

stories.title: Iran says it has closed the Strait of Hormuz again, as ceasefire nears its end
stories.description: Iran's military said on Saturday the Strait of Hormuz has "returned to its previous state." The announcement came after President Trump had said the blockade on Iranian ports would remain in place.

stories.title: French peacekeeper killed in southern Lebanon
stories.description: President Macron blames the attack on Hezbollah. The Iran-backed armed group denies "any connection" to the incident.

stories.title: A moment of risk and opportunity: How two ceasefires could boost US-Iran talks
stories.description: Some kind of diplomatic progress is now under way, but is it enough to prevent a return to war?

stories.title: Ceasefire with Israel brings respite to Lebanon, but obstacles to peace remain
stories.description: The halt to weeks of fighting is being celebrated in Lebanon, but it will be difficult to ensure it lasts.

stories.title: What we know about the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire
stories.description: A 10-day truce between the two countries is now in effect, with Iran-backed Hezbollah voicing support, as negotiations continue between the US and Iran.

stories.title: Lyse Doucet: Under fragile ceasefire, Iranians wonder if US deal can be done
stories.description: The BBC's chief international correspondent reports from Iran as diplomatic efforts to avoid a return to war intensify.

stories.title: UK and France to lead defensive mission in Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Sir Keir Starmer has said further details of the plan will come after a meeting in London next week.

stories.title: Israeli triple-tap strike kills three paramedics in Lebanon, officials say
stories.description: Lebanon's government condemns as a "flagrant crime" the killing of the paramedics, one of whom featured in a BBC report.

stories.title: Sri Lanka repatriates 238 Iranian sailors stranded after US torpedo attack
stories.description: The survivors of the US attack on Iranian vessel Iris Dena, which claimed the lives of 104 people, were among those sent home.

stories.title: The Israeli town on the frontline with Hezbollah
stories.description: BBC foreign correspondent Nick Beake visits Metula, an Israeli town surrounded on three sides by Lebanon.

stories.title: Why and how is US blockading Iranian ports in Strait of Hormuz?
stories.description: Donald Trump says that the US is blockading the Strait of Hormuz. What does this mean in practice?

stories.title: Middle East: Hezbollah blamed after French peacekeeper killed in Lebanon
stories.description: A French peacekeeper serving with United Nations Interim Force in Lebanon was killed and three others wounded in an ambush in southern Lebanon, with Emmanuel Macron blaming Hezbollah, which denies responsibility.

stories.title: What is UNIFIL? the UN mission in southern Lebanon explained
stories.description: The United Nations Interim Force in Lebanon (UNIFIL) has patrolled southern Lebanon along the border with Israel since 1978, acting as a buffer force following Israel’s invasion of the region. Expanded after the 2006 war between Israel and Hezbollah, the mission now monitors the ...

stories.title: Middle East war live: Strait of Hormuz stays shut as Iran reports 'progress' in US talks
stories.description: Iran’s parliamentary speaker said Saturday night that talks with the United States to end the war had made progress but remained far from an agreement, as Tehran said the crucial Strait of Hormuz would remain closed until Washington lifts its naval blockade on Iranian ports. Foll...

stories.title: Strait of Hormuz: Open, closed, open, closed - What's actually happening?
stories.description: The confusion around the Strait of Hormuz continues at pace. Having opened the straight on Friday for commercial vessels, Iran has moved to close the crucial channel once again. Tehran has accused the United States of "piracy" and added that any ship approaching the straights wil...

stories.title: French UNIFIL soldier killed in Lebanon, Hezbollah responsible, says Macron
stories.description: French President Emmanuel Macron announced the death of a French UN peacekeeping soldier Saturday following an attack that also left three people wounded in southern Lebanon, saying that "everything suggests" Hezbollah was responsible for the attack. The Iran-backed group denied ...

stories.title: Replay : Donald Trump holds briefing amid Hormuz tension
stories.description: Rewatch Donald Trump’s live briefing from the White House on Saturday, April 18, as Iran announced it was re-closing the Strait of Hormuz while the US maintains its port blockade. FRANCE 24's Andrew Hilliar has the analysis.

stories.title: UNIFIL sheds light on southern Lebanon attack that killed French soldier
stories.description: A French soldier was killed in southern Lebanon on Saturday 18, while serving with UNIFIL, the United Nations peacekeeping force. UNIFIL deputy spokesperson Tilak Pokharel was FRANCE 24’s guest this afternoon to explain what happened and what is currently known about those respon...

stories.title: No signs of regime collapse as Iran weathers weeks of war
stories.description: After weeks of US and Israeli strikes, Iranians are watching to see if ceasefire talks could lead to negotiations, while uncertainty over the future of the conflict persists. Despite foreign calls for regime change, analysts say Iran’s leadership remains entrenched, as economic p...

stories.title: How the Israeli military targets the people who save lives
stories.description: Israeli attacks have killed at least 57 medical workers in Lebanon since early March. It is a pattern lifted from Gaza.

stories.title: Iran, US still ‘far’ from breakthrough amid Strait of Hormuz impasse
stories.description: Tehran is 'fully prepared' for the US to resume hostilities at any moment, says Iran's parliament speaker Ghalibaf.

stories.title: Iran war live: Tehran says no date set for US talks, Hormuz Strait closed
stories.description: IRGC says the Strait of Hormuz will remain closed until the US stops blockading Iranian ports.

stories.title: Iran reasserts control of Hormuz Strait as Trump warns against ‘blackmail’
stories.description: Top negotiator Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf says US naval blockade of Iran's ports is 'a clumsy and ignorant decision'.

stories.title: Iran military orders Indian ship to abort Strait of Hormuz passage
stories.description: Video shows the moment Iran’s military ordered an Indian ship to abort its passage of the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Israel says established a ‘yellow line’ in Lebanon, as it has in Gaza
stories.description: It is the first time Israel has referred to such a 'yellow line' in Lebanon, after using a similar measure in Gaza.

stories.title: Mazzucato on the Iran war’s economic shock: Who pays the price?
stories.description: Redi Tlhabi speaks to economist Mariana Mazzucato on the Iran war's economic fallout and who’s really paying the price.

stories.title: 17 immigrants have died in US custody this year: ICE
stories.description: WASHINGTON: At least 17 immigrants have died in US Immigration and Customs Enforcement custody from January 2026 through early April, according to ICE. That follows 31 deaths last year, a two-decade high. Aled Damien Carbonell-Betan­court of Cuba died on April 12 at a federal det...

stories.title: France blames Hezbollah for soldier’s death in Lebanon
stories.description: • Hezbollah denies allegation, calls for ‘caution in making judgements’• Macron urges Beirut to ‘guarantee security’ of UNIFIL peacekeepers PARIS / BEIRUT: A French soldier was killed and three others wounded in an attack on Saturday on UN peacekeepers in Lebanon that appeared to...

stories.title: Mass rallies in Germany demand faster shift to renewable energy
stories.description: BERLIN: Thousands of people demonstrated acr­oss Germany on Saturday, urging a faster shift to re­­newable energy and accusing conservative Chancel­lor Friedrich Merz’s coalition of putting the brakes on the transition. In Berlin, Cologne, Hamburg and Munich, crowds took to the s...

stories.title: Pope Leo downplays feud with Trump, says ‘not in my interest’ to debate him
stories.description: Pope Leo sought to downplay his feud with US President Donald Trump on Saturday, saying reporting about comments he has made so far during his Africa tour “has not been accurate in all its aspects”. Speaking to reporters in English aboard his flight to Angola for the third leg of...

stories.title: New Delhi summons Iranian envoy after Indian-flagged tankers shot at near Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: New Delhi on Saturday summoned Iran’s ambassador to convey the country’s “deep concern” after two Indian-flagged vessels were shot at near the Strait of Hormuz, India’s foreign ministry said. In a statement, India’s Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) said Iran’s Ambassador to Ind...

stories.title: Iran reimposes control over Strait of Hormuz as ships report gunfire
stories.description: A handful of oil and gas tankers crossed the Strait of Hormuz on Saturday during a brief reopening, tracking data showed, but others retreated and two were reportedly attacked as Iran closed the route again. The toing and froing over the strait cast doubt on US President Donald T...

stories.title: Iran-Israel war LIVE: 'Bold and loyal', Trump praises Israel as 'great ally' of U.S.
stories.description: In a significant show of diplomatic support, U.S. President Donald Trump has issued a robust endorsement of Israel, characterising the nation as a premier strategic partner during periods of heightened regional instability

stories.title: Bangladesh raises fuel prices as conflict in West Asia drives up costs
stories.description: Officials ⁠said the increase was unavoidable ⁠as rising crude prices, supply chain disruptions and higher freight and insurance costs pushed up import expenses in ​recent weeks, particularly after ‌oil prices have jumped during the seven-week-old war on Iran.

stories.title: India ‘calls in’ Iranian envoy, conveys ‘deep concern’ after two oil tankers prevented from crossing Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) describes the meeting as the Ambassador being “called in”, not “summoned”

stories.title: Trump vs Pope | Two Americans, two paths
stories.description: The 70-year-old pontiff, leader of 1.4 billion Catholics, has spoken out against the American President over the U.S.-Israel war on Iran, saying that in a world ‘being ravaged by a handful of tyrants’, peace ‘is everyone’s responsibility’

stories.title: Iran fully closes Strait of Hormuz over U.S. blockade
stories.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guard navy warned vessels not to move from their anchorage in the Persian Gulf and the Sea of Oman, as “approaching the Strait of Hormuz will be considered as cooperation with the enemy"

stories.title: Iran-Israel war updates: Iran fully closes Strait of Hormuz over U.S. blockade, fires on ships
stories.description: Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri met Iran’s ambassador to India and conveyed “deep concern” after two India-bound energy-carrying vessels were prevented from transiting the Strait of Hormuz

stories.title: Israeli army announces death of another soldier in Lebanon, nine wounded
stories.description: A reserve sergeant from the 769th Territorial Brigade was killed in the combat zone

stories.title: Iran war Trump’s ‘feeble attempt’ to distract attention from Epstein case — Kamala Harris
stories.description: According to the politician, the United States was "drawn into" the conflict by Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu

stories.title: Belarus ready for big deal with US, but it needs to be prepared — president
stories.description: Alexander Lukashenko emphasized that Western sanctions had not played their part

stories.title: US sanctions relief on Russian oil extension important but not critical for India — expert
stories.description: However, it is not critical, according to a researcher and senior advisor at the Indian Institute of Energy and Resources
